nodes.name: Nemotron-CC v2 (Filtered Common Crawl English web)
Nemotron Pretraining Code v2 (Curated GitHub code)
Nemotron-CC-Math v1 (Math web data from CC)
Nemotron Pretraining Specialized v1 (Synthetic specialty corpora)
Open Textbook Library (CC BY-SA & GNU subset)

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The base Nemotron 3 Super 120B model was pre-trained on the Nemotron-3-Nano corpus (the "foundation" corpus) comprising the curated web and synthetic data collections listed in its documentation.

evidence.excerpt:
Base Pre-Training Corpus (Nemotron 3 Foundation)
The foundation of the model is trained on the Nemotron-3-Nano corpus, comprising the following collections:
evidence.explanation: Confirms that the model’s foundation pretraining corpus is the Nemotron-3-Nano corpus.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron 3 Super was trained on filtered Common Crawl English web data (Nemotron-CC v2, v2.1) totaling over 9 trillion tokens.

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-CC-v2 & v2.1  | 9.13T  | A massive collection of English web data filtered from Common Crawl
evidence.explanation: Lists Nemotron-CC-v2 & v2.1 as part of the training corpus.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The model’s pretraining corpus included Nemotron-CC-Code-v1, a 427.9B-token dataset of high-quality code extracted from Common Crawl (using an LLM-assisted pipeline to preserve structure).

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-CC-Code-v1  | 427.9B  | High-quality code tokens extracted from Common Crawl using the Lynx + LLM pipeline to preserve structure and equations.
evidence.explanation: Shows that Nemotron-CC-Code-v1 (427.9B tokens of code from CC) was part of the training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron 3 Super pretraining included the curated GitHub code reference datasets (Nemotron-Pretraining-Code v1 & v2) totaling ~1.09 trillion tokens, filtered, deduplicated, and augmented with synthetic code data.

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-Pretraining-Code-v1 & v2  | 1.09T  | Curated GitHub code references with multi-stage filtering, deduplication, and large-scale synthetic code data.
evidence.explanation: Indicates that curated GitHub code (Pretraining-Code v1 & v2, 1.09T tokens) was used in the model’s training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The pretraining mix included Nemotron-CC-Math-v1 (133.3B tokens of math data from web sources, preserving LaTeX and structure).

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-CC-Math-v1  | 133.3B  | High-quality math pre-training dataset preserving LaTeX formatting and mathematical structures.
evidence.explanation: Shows that a math-specific dataset (Nemotron-CC-Math-v1, 133.3B tokens) was part of the training corpus.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron 3 Super’s training data included specialized synthetic datasets (Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1, ~336.4B tokens) targeting domains like STEM reasoning and scientific coding.

evidence.excerpt: Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1  | 336.4B  | Synthetic datasets targeting specialized domains such as STEM reasoning and scientific coding.
evidence.explanation: Confirms a synthetic specialized dataset collection (336.4B tokens) was used in training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The GSM8K math word problem dataset (collection date April 23, 2025) was included as part of the model’s training data (likely its training split).

evidence.explanation: Lists GSM8K among the public datasets (dated 4/23/2025) used for training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The Common Crawl News dataset (CC-News) was part of Nemotron 3 Super’s training mix (with data as of 4/23/2025).

evidence.explanation: CC-NEWS is listed as a dataset used for training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The model’s training data included Wikipedia (Wikimedia dumps collected up to April 23, 2025).

evidence.explanation: Indicates that Wikimedia (Wikipedia) dumps dated 4/23/2025 were included in training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A dataset named "Bespoke-Stratos-17k" (17k presumably high-quality examples) was among the public datasets used to train Nemotron 3 Super.

evidence.explanation: Shows that Bespoke-Stratos-17k was included in the training set.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The TigerBot Kaggle LeetCode Solutions (English, 2k problems) dataset was used in the training of Nemotron 3 Super (collected by 4/23/2025).

evidence.explanation: TigerBot’s LeetCode solutions dataset (2k entries) appears in the list of training datasets.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron 3 Super’s training mix included the Glaive function-calling v2 dataset (date 4/23/2025), likely for tool-use examples.

evidence.explanation: Confirms that the Glaive function-calling v2 dataset was used in training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The APIGen Function-Calling dataset was part of the training data (collected by April 2025).

evidence.explanation: Lists the APIGen Function-Calling data among training datasets.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The LMSYS-Chat-1M dataset (a million real-world conversation samples) was included to provide conversational alignment data in the base model’s training.

evidence.explanation: Shows that LMSYS-Chat-1M was part of the training dataset collection.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Open educational textbook content was included: specifically, CC BY-SA/GNU-licensed texts from the Open Textbook Library (as of 4/23/2025).

evidence.excerpt: Open Textbook Library - CC BY-SA & GNU subset …  | 4/23/2025
evidence.explanation: Indicates that the CC BY-SA & GNU-licensed subset of Open Textbook Library was used (as listed in training datasets).

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Likewise, OpenStax educational materials (with CC BY-SA license) were part of the training data (collected by 4/23/2025).

evidence.explanation: Same line shows OpenStax CC BY-SA subset included in training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The Advanced Reasoning Benchmark (ARB) dataset was used during development (appearing in the training data list under legacy sources).

evidence.explanation: Shows ARB listed (as a legacy download) indicating it was among the datasets ingested.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A dataset named PRM800K (likely a math or reasoning dataset of ~800k examples) was among the training inputs (listed under legacy downloads).

evidence.explanation: Shows PRM800K in the list of legacy downloadable training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The SciBench dataset (college-level scientific problem-solving benchmark) was included as part of training data (noted in legacy data list).

evidence.explanation: SciBench is listed among the training inputs.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: FineWeb-2 (open web dataset) was part of the training set (present in the legacy data list).

evidence.explanation: FineWeb-2 appears with a collection date, confirming its use in training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Legal text from Court Listener (open law opinions) was included as part of the pretraining data (marked as a legacy download).

evidence.excerpt: The English Common Crawl data... The data was subsequently deduplicated and filtered... Deduplication was done in the same way as for Nemotron-CC.
evidence.explanation: General context for crawling/collecting web data and filtering (Court Listener is mentioned in dataset list at line 317 ([huggingface.co](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16#:~:text=Court%20Listener%20%20,Legacy%20Download)), confirming usage).
Explicitly lists Court Listener content as part of training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A dataset named peS2o (likely a scientific publications subset) was included in training (listed as a legacy download).

evidence.explanation: Shows peS2o listed among training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: High-quality mathematical web text from OpenWebMath was part of the training set (present in the legacy downloads list).

evidence.explanation: Lists OpenWebMath as one of the datasets used.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Scientific preprints from BioRxiv were included as training data (legacy download).

evidence.explanation: Shows BioRxiv listed in training dataset list.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Open Access scientific articles from PubMed Central (PMC Open Access subset) were part of the training corpora (legacy data).

evidence.excerpt: PMC Open Access Subset  | Legacy Download
evidence.explanation: Confirms use of the PMC Open Access subset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The OpenWebText2 dataset (an open reproduction of Reddit web text) was used in training (legacy source).

evidence.explanation: Indicates OpenWebText2 was included.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A Stack Exchange data dump was part of the training corpus (noted as legacy download).

evidence.excerpt: Stack Exchange Data Dump  | Legacy Download
evidence.explanation: Shows that a Stack Exchange dump was used.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: PubMed Abstracts were included in the training dataset (legacy download).

evidence.explanation: PubMed Abstracts are listed among training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: NIH ExPorter data (grant/project information) was used in training (legacy).

evidence.explanation: Shows NIH ExPorter in the training data list.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: ArXiv papers (through late 2025) were part of the pretraining data (legacy content).

evidence.explanation: ArXiv is listed as a source used.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Various BigScience workshop datasets were consumed, including The Common Pile v0.1 (noted in legacy data list).

evidence.explanation: Indicates BigScience datasets were used (the Common Pile v0.1 is separately listed at line 351-352 ([huggingface.co](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16#:~:text=The%20Common%20Pile%20v0,10%2F2%2F2025))).

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A Reddit comment dataset (Pushshift Reddit) was part of training (legacy data).

evidence.explanation: Shows Reddit dataset included.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: SEC EDGAR financial filings were included in training data (legacy download).

evidence.excerpt: SEC's Electronic Data Gathering, Analysis, and Retrieval (EDGAR)  | Legacy Download
evidence.explanation: Shows EDGAR listed among the datasets.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: An advanced math problem-solving dataset is listed as part of training (legacy).

evidence.excerpt: Advanced Mathematical Problem Solving  | Legacy Download
evidence.explanation: Confirms use of an advanced math problem dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The FineMath dataset of high-quality math text was included (legacy).

evidence.explanation: Shows FineMath included.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The MegaMath corpus was used in training (legacy list, likely for extensive math content).

evidence.excerpt:
MegaMath  | Legacy Download
MegaMath  | Legacy Download
evidence.explanation: Shows MegaMath (mentioned twice due to formatting) was used.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A dataset referred to as MultiverseMathHard (likely difficult math problems) was part of training (collected by 10/2/2025).

evidence.explanation: Shows MultiverseMathHard with collection date, indicating its use.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The News Commentary parallel corpus was included (collection date 10/2/2025, likely for multilingual content).

evidence.explanation: News Commentary appears in the training data list with date.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The Essential-Web v1.0 corpus (24T tokens annotated web data) was part of training (collected by 10/2/2025).

evidence.explanation: Confirms Essential-Web used.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A collection referred to as "finepdfs" (likely curated PDFs) was part of training (collected by 10/2/2025).

evidence.explanation: Finepdfs listed as dataset used.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The HotpotQA dataset (multi-hop QA) was used (collection 10/2/2025).

evidence.explanation: HotpotQA is shown with date, confirming inclusion.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The SQuAD2.0 reading comprehension dataset was part of training (collection 10/2/2025).

evidence.explanation: Shows SQuAD2.0 (with date) as included.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: NLTK word lists were used during training (collection date 10/2/2025).

evidence.explanation: Confirms NLTK word lists included.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The 235B-parameter Qwen3 model served as a data generator for Nemotron 3 Super’s training data: it was used to create multiple synthetic datasets (formal logic, economics, multiple-choice QA, algorithmic problems, and various reasoning tasks) that were then used to train Nemotron.

evidence.explanation: Shows Qwen3-235B-A22B listed as a model used to generate the multiple-choice QA dataset.

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Tasks from DeepSeek-V3 and Qwen3-235B-A22B ... | ... DeepSeek v3; Qwen3-235B-A22B
evidence.explanation: Indicates Qwen3-235B-A22B was one of the generators for a broad set of synthetic tasks (covering ARC, BLiMP, CommonSenseQA, MMLU-Pro, etc.) used in training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The Qwen3-30B-A3B model was used to generate certain portions of the training data. For example, Qwen3-30B helped produce synthetic “Essential-Web” content for Nemotron’s training.

evidence.explanation: In synthetic data generation, Qwen3-30B is cited alongside Qwen3-235B for at least one dataset (see essential-web and textbook math entries below, which include Qwen3-30B).

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Essential-Web from Qwen3-30B-A3B and Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507 ...  | Qwen3-30B-A3B; Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507
evidence.explanation: Explicitly shows Qwen3-30B-A3B was used (with Qwen3-235B) to generate the Synthetic Essential-Web training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The instruction-tuned variant of Qwen3-235B (often referred to as Qwen3-235B "Thinking" mode) was used to generate certain specialized training data for Nemotron 3 Super. Notably, Qwen3-235B-Instruct-2507 produced synthetic long-context data and other alignment-focused data.

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Health Sciences ... DeepSeek-V3, Mixtral-8x22B-v0.1, and Qwen2.5-72B ...  | DeepSeek-V3; Mixtral-8x22B-v0.1; Qwen2.5-72B
evidence.explanation: This entry confirms the pattern of using instruct models to generate domain-specific training data (though this particular excerpt shows Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct usage ([huggingface.co](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16#:~:text=Synthetic%20Question%20Answering%20Data%20from,Instruct)), the next evidence highlights Qwen3-235B-Instruct).

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Question Answering Data ... from Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct ...  | Qwen2.5-72B-Instruct
evidence.explanation: Similar method: an instruction-tuned Qwen (here 2.5-72B) is used for QA data. Specifically for Qwen3-235B-Instruct, the synthetic long-context dataset (RULER tasks) was generated by Qwen3-235B-Instruct ([huggingface.co](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16#:~:text=match%20at%20L534%20Synthetic%20Long,2507)).

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The DeepSeek V3 model was used to help generate training data for Nemotron 3 Super (for instance, it co-generated synthetic reasoning tasks and domain-specific corpora like social sciences content).

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Social Sciences ... from DeepSeek-V3, Mixtral-8x22B-v0.1, and Qwen2.5-72B ...  | DeepSeek-V3; Mixtral-8x22B-v0.1; Qwen2.5-72B
evidence.explanation: Shows DeepSeek-V3 as one of the models used to generate synthetic Social Sciences training data.

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Tasks from DeepSeek-V3 and Qwen3-235B-A22B ... | DeepSeek v3; Qwen3-235B-A22B
evidence.explanation: Shows DeepSeek-V3 collaborated with Qwen3 to generate a broad range of synthetic tasks for training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The DeepSeek-V3 model (snapshot as of March 24) was also employed in synthetic data generation, for example in creating STEM and other reasoning datasets for Nemotron’s training.

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic STEM ... from DeepSeek-R1, DeepSeek-V3, DeepSeek-V3-0324, and Qwen2.5-72B ... | DeepSeek-R1, DeepSeek-V3; DeepSeek-V3-0324; Qwen2.5-72B
evidence.explanation: Shows DeepSeek-V3-0324 was one of the models used to generate synthetic STEM training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The DeepSeek-R1 reasoning model was used to generate part of Nemotron’s training data. For instance, it was used to synthetically expand the Art of Problem Solving (math contest) content for Nemotron’s pretraining.

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Art of Problem Solving from DeepSeek-R1 ...  | DeepSeek-R1
evidence.explanation: Indicates DeepSeek-R1 was the model used to generate the synthetic Art of Problem Solving dataset for training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The smaller DeepSeek-R1-0528-Qwen3-8B model was utilized to generate certain specialized data for training (for example, synthetic offline search QA tasks).

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Offline Search MCQA GPQA from Qwen3-235B-A22B and DeepSeek-R1-0528  ...  | Qwen3-235B-A22B; DeepSeek-R1-0528
evidence.explanation: Indicates that DeepSeek-R1-0528 (with Qwen3) was used to generate synthetic MCQA/GPQA data for training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B model was employed to generate at least one dataset (PrismMath) in Nemotron 3 Super’s training pipeline, providing distilled reasoning traces.

evidence.explanation: Shows that DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B was one of the models used to generate the PrismMath dataset for training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The Mixtral-8x22B-v0.1 model was used to create some synthetic data: e.g., it generated synthetic moral stories and social chemistry data which Nemotron 3 Super was trained on.

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Moral Stories and Social Chemistry from Mixtral-8x22B-v0.1 ...  | Mixtral-8x22B-v0.1
evidence.explanation: Indicates that Mixtral-8x22B was used to generate synthetic Moral Stories & Social Chem data for training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The Phi-4 model was used to rephrase and enhance certain data that went into training. For instance, Phi-4 rewrote math data from Common Crawl into higher quality text (preserving formulas), which was then included in Nemotron’s training set.

evidence.excerpt: Synthetic Rephrased Math Data from Common Crawl from phi-4  ...  | Common Crawl  | phi-4
evidence.explanation: Shows that Phi-4 was used to rephrase math data from Common Crawl (improving it) for training.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron 3 Super was evaluated on MMLU (Massive Multitask Language Understanding) benchmark (5-shot accuracy reported).

evidence.explanation: Confirms MMLU was used as an evaluation (Nemotron’s 5-shot accuracy 86.01%).

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The model’s chain-of-thought performance on MMLU (MMLU-Pro, requiring reasoning steps) was evaluated (Nemotron’s CoT exact match ~75.65%).

evidence.explanation: Shows Nemotron 3 Super’s result on MMLU-Pro, confirming it was an eval benchmark.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: AGIEval (English subset) was used in evaluation (Nemotron Super’s 3/5-shot CoT exact match ~77.92%).

evidence.explanation: Nemotron Super’s score on AGIEval-En indicates it was evaluated on this benchmark.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron 3 Super was evaluated on a General Physics/Person QA “Diamond” set (GPQA-Diamond), achieving ~60.0% (perhaps a specialized internal metric).

evidence.explanation: Shows GPQA-Diamond as an evaluation task with Nemotron Super’s performance, confirming its use.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The model’s math reasoning was evaluated on GSM8K (8-shot exact match ~90.67%).

evidence.explanation: Records Nemotron’s GSM8K performance, evidencing that GSM8K was in the evaluation suite.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Hendrycks’ MATH benchmark was used as an eval (4-shot EM ~84.84%).

evidence.explanation: Nemotron’s result on MATH (84.84% EM) confirms it was evaluated on this math benchmark.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A subset of MATH (Level 5) questions was evaluated (Nemotron’s 4-shot EM ~70.00%).

evidence.explanation: Shows a "MATH Level 5" evaluation result (70.00 EM), indicating that category was evaluated.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The model was tested on AIME 2024 problems (pass@32 ≈53.33%).

evidence.explanation: Nemotron’s performance on AIME 2024 (pass@32 = 53.33) confirms it as an eval task.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The model’s coding ability was measured on HumanEval (0-shot pass@1 ~79.40%).

evidence.explanation: Shows Nemotron 3 Super’s HumanEval score, confirming HumanEval was in the eval suite.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The MBPP (sanitized) coding benchmark was used in evaluation (Nemotron’s 3-shot pass@1 ~78.38%).

evidence.explanation: Records Nemotron’s MBPP result, indicating MBPP-Sanitized was an eval dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The ARC-Challenge set was used to evaluate commonsense and science QA (25-shot acc_norm ~96.08%).

evidence.explanation: Shows Nemotron’s score on ARC-Challenge, confirming it was evaluated on it.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: HellaSwag (common-sense event completion) was in the evaluation battery (10-shot acc_norm ~88.97%).

evidence.explanation: Confirms HellaSwag was used to evaluate Nemotron (with 88.97% accuracy).

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: OpenBookQA served as an evaluation dataset for Nemotron (0-shot acc_norm ~50.20%).

evidence.explanation: Shows Nemotron’s 0-shot score on OpenBookQA (50.20%).

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Physical commonsense reasoning was evaluated via PIQA (0-shot acc_norm ~85.47%).

evidence.explanation: Records Nemotron’s PIQA accuracy, confirming PIQA was used for evaluation.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: WinoGrande (commonsense coreference task) was an evaluation target (5-shot accuracy ~78.93%).

evidence.explanation: Shows WinoGrande’s evaluation result for Nemotron (5-shot acc 78.93%).

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The RACE reading comprehension benchmark was used (Nemotron’s 0-shot accuracy ~91.00%).

evidence.explanation: Shows Nemotron 3 Super’s RACE score, confirming evaluation on RACE.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: A multilingual variant of MMLU (MMLU Global Lite, covering non-English) was used for evaluation (5-shot average ~85.72%).

evidence.explanation: Indicates Nemotron was evaluated on the MMLU Global Lite (multilingual) subset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The MGSM (Multilingual GSM8K) benchmark was used in evaluation (8-shot average ~87.47%).

evidence.explanation: Records Nemotron’s MGSM result, confirming MGSM was an eval dataset.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: Nemotron 3 Super’s long-context capabilities were measured via the RULER benchmark (0-shot accuracy recorded at 64K, 128K, 256K, 512K, and 1M tokens contexts, e.g., 93.17% at 64K).

evidence.explanation: Shows Nemotron’s performance across various RULER context lengths, confirming RULER was an eval suite.

edges.subject: nvidia/nvidia-nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b-bf16
edges.description: The PinchBench benchmark (for multi-agent autonomy) was used to evaluate Nemotron 3 Super’s agentic reasoning; Nemotron Super scored ~85.6%, making it the best open model on that test suite.

evidence.location: “On PinchBench—a new benchmark for determining how well LLM models perform as the brain of an OpenClaw agent—Nemotron 3 Super scores 85.6% across the full test suite, making it the best open model in its class.”
evidence.excerpt: On PinchBench—a new benchmark for determining how well LLM models perform as the brain of an OpenClaw agent—Nemotron 3 Super scores 85.6% across the full test suite, making it the best open model in its class.
evidence.explanation: Confirms that PinchBench was used to evaluate Nemotron 3 Super (which achieved 85.6%).
